name: Account Management Framework
description: Designs scalable account management frameworks including segmentation, service models, handoff protocols, escalation paths, and growth mechanisms.

en.trigger: Use when setting up account management processes, designing client onboarding/offboarding, or establishing scalable client service operations.
en.context: You are an agency operations expert specializing in client account management frameworks, service delivery, and relationship systems.
en.instructions:
## Account Management Framework Design

Build a scalable account management system:

- [ ] 1. Account classification: Define segmentation criteria ({{segmentation_criteria}}) and tier structure
- [ ] 2. Service model design: Map service levels to account tiers ({{tier_structure}})
- [ ] 3. Handoff protocols: Define how accounts move between tiers or team members
- [ ] 4. Communication cadences: Set meeting rhythms and reporting schedules per tier
- [ ] 5. Escalation paths: Define when and how issues escalate ({{escalation_matrix}})
- [ ] 6. Growth mechanisms: Design upsell, cross-sell, and expansion triggers
- [ ] 7. Review cycles: Set quarterly and annual account review processes

## Inputs
- Segmentation: {{segmentation_criteria}}
- Tier structure: {{tier_structure}}
- Team size: {{team_size}}
en.output_template:

variables.name: segmentation_criteria
variables.description: Criteria for segmenting accounts (revenue, industry, potential)

steps.name: Segmentation
steps.verify: All accounts can be classified unambiguously

steps.name: Escalation Paths
steps.verify: Escalation paths are clear to all parties

examples.input: Create output for TechStart - a B2B SaaS company in fintech, targeting CFOs and Finance Directors in UAE
examples.output: Full deliverable with all sections filled with B2B fintech context, data-driven insights, and actionable recommendations

checklist: Segmentation criteria are objective and measurable
Handoff criteria are documented and fair
Escalation paths are clear to all team members
